channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Woman threatens insurance company, says 'Delay, Deny, Depose': Police
item.description: Near the end of the call, investigators said Boston could be heard stating, "Delay, Deny, Depose. You people are next.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, NewsNation

item.title: Biden commutes roughly 1,500 sentences and pardons 39 people in biggest single-day act of clemency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is commuting the sentences of roughly 1,500 people who were released from prison and placed on home confinement during the coronavirus pandemic and is pardoning 39 Americans convicted of nonviolent crimes.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Funeral for activist turns into call for justice for other missing Syrians
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Hundreds of Syrians marched through central Damascus in a funeral procession Thursday to honor one of the country's most prominent anti-government activists, whose body was found after the ouster of President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Black denomination upholds stance against same-sex marriage. A gay pastor in its ranks seeks change
item.description: When the African Methodist Episcopal Church, arguably the world’s largest independent Black Protestant denomination, held its quadrennial General Conference in Ohio in August, among the agenda items was an issue that the Rev. Jennifer S. Leath had labored over for two decades: same-sex marriage.
item.dc:creator: DARREN SANDS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: House sends Biden bill adding judicial seats after veto threat
item.description: The House approved a bill Thursday that would create dozens of additional judicial seats over the next several years, sending it to President Biden’s desk after his administration levied a veto threat.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9

item.title: FAA administrator to resign on Trump swearing-in
item.description: Mike Whitaker is leaving at the start of President-elect Trump’s term, a source familiar with his plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Higher coffee prices could be brewing for consumers as the global coffee market smashed a 47-year record.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump again named Time's Person of the Year
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump rang the opening bell Thursday at the New York Stock Exchange after being recognized for the second time by Time magazine as its Person of the Year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mysterious drone flights over New Jersey: Homeland Security shares new details
item.description: The large mysterious drones reported flying over parts of New Jersey in recent weeks appear to avoid detection by traditional methods such as helicopter and radio, according to a state lawmaker briefed Wednesday by the Department of Homeland Security.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Shipkowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Clinton says he’s open to talking to Biden about preemptive pardon for Hillary Clinton
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton said he is open to having a conversation with President Joe Biden about a potential preemptive pardon for his wife, former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rural towns push to divorce from big cities, form new conservative state
item.description: New move to secede from blue parts of California is being pushed by group hoping to created a new state away from the Democratic-controlled Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ivy League murder suspect's alma mater UPenn silent on CEO assassination after professor's celebratory post
item.description: The University of Pennsylvania has refused to comment on alum Luigi Mangione's arrest or to condemn the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turning dusty attic treasures into cash can yield millions for some and disappointment for others
item.description: THOMASTON, Maine (AP) — Kaja Veilleux has been hunting New England attic treasures for more than 50 years.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: East Coast storm makes a mess at ski resorts as strong winds cause power outages
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Colder weather began to return Thursday after a storm that swept up the East Coast delivered a blow to New England, packing powerful gusts that knocked out power along with a deluge of rain and warming temperatures that washed away snow and dampened ski resorts.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Ivy League grad suspect spawns movement fueled by anti-capitalist sentiment
item.description: Speculation about a potential motive in the murder of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson has focused on the suspect's possible anti-capitalist sentiment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Atmospheric river brings weather whiplash to East Coast as bomb cyclone develops
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The U.S. East Coast began a whiplash-inducing stretch of weather Wednesday with a deluge of rain, rapid snowmelt and powerful gusts, creating dangerous conditions, due in part to an atmospheric river and developing bomb cyclone.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire danger diminishes in Southern California even as crews continue to battle Malibu blaze
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — As weather improved in Southern California, firefighters found some success Wednesday battling a wind-driven blaze burning in steep, nearly inaccessible areas that forced thousands, including celebrities, from their homes in Malibu, fire officials said.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JAE C. HONG and GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Aftermath of small plane crash onto Texas roadway caught on camera with split fuselage, damaged vehicles
item.description: The FAA and the Victoria Police Department in Texas said a small plane crashed into a busy intersection, colliding with three vehicles Wednesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin kayaker who faked his death and fled the country is charged after returning to US
item.description: GREEN LAKE, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin man who faked his own drowning and left his wife and three children for Eastern Europe willingly returned to the U.S. after four months and was charged Wednesday with obstructing an intense lake search for his body.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Private Sin: How a Michigan church protected child molesters
item.description: Protestant Reformed Churches in America is struggling with what it has called the "private sin" of child sexual abuse and decades of covering up for abusers, including church leaders and school teachers.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon Ducks 'screwed' by CFP committee, Fox Sports analyst says
item.description: Joel Klatt, Fox Sports top college football analyst, blasted the CFP committee, claiming they did a "horrendous" job with their selections.
item.dc:creator: Matt Rawlings, NewsNation

item.title: Albertsons sues Kroger for failing to win approval of their proposed supermarket merger
item.description: Kroger and Albertsons' plan for the largest U.S. supermarket merger in history crumbled Wednesday, with Albertsons pulling out of the $24.6 billion deal and the two companies accusing each other of not doing enough to push their proposed alliance through.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Police shut down 'The Conjuring' house, cite business for operating without license
item.description: Police responded to the famous home after seeing customers showing up even though the business lost its license.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Guernelli, NewsNation

item.title: Grave of famed abolitionist designated L.A. County historical landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles County Board of Supervisors unanimously voted Tuesday to designate the gravesite of abolitionist Owen Brown as a Los Angeles County Historical Landmark, marking the conclusion of a nearly yearlong process.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: The Amazing Kreskin, renowned mentalist and frequent late-night guest, dies at 89
item.description: The Amazing Kreskin, an entertainer and mentalist who rose to fame amid frequent appearances on TV talk shows like “The Tonight Show” and “The Mike Douglas Show,” has died, his family confirmed on social media. He was 89 years old.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Dick Van Dyke, Cher among those forced to flee Malibu homes in wake of wildfire
item.description: Officials said the fire, which grew significantly overnight, was only 7% contained as of Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: Inflation ticks higher as Fed eyes another rate cut
item.description: Inflation picked up slightly in November, according to data released Wednesday by the Labor Department, but likely not by enough to derail the Federal Reserve's plans for another rate cut.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: How to pick the perfect Windows laptop
item.description: Shopping for a new computer can be intimidating. There are so many models to choose from and so many specs to decide on. "They get really excited when they come in here.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Friends, acquaintances defend Mangione family as media descends on alleged CEO killer's hometown
item.description: Alleged CEO killer Luigi Mangione's family has a deep footprint in the state of Maryland, and friends and acquaintances of the Mangiones say they were shocked at the news of Luigi's arrest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oregon federal judge temporarily blocks proposed Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: Oregon was one of several states to join the Federal Trade Commission’s lawsuit that aimed to block the merger.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, Fox 5

item.title: A fugitive gains fame in New Orleans eluding dart guns and nets
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A scruffy little fugitive is on the lam again in New Orleans, gaining fame as he outwits a tenacious band of citizens armed with night-vision binoculars, nets and a tranquilizer rifle.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4
